summary: End-to-end testing of ride refusal flow (POST /api/rides/{ride_id}/refuse + refused_by filtering + frontend dismissRide). All 7 backend tests PASS (100%). UI flow verified: 'Refuser' button triggers API call, ride disappears for refusing driver, remains visible for other drivers, toast 'Course refusée - Elle sera proposée à un autre chauffeur' shown.

issues: 'Géolocalisation requise' warning shown even when driver is marked online via API - acceptable since UI prompts browser geo permission, but worth noting

passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/rides/available correctly filters out rides where current driver is in refused_by
Backend: Refused ride remains visible to OTHER drivers (refused_by is driver-specific)
Backend: /refuse returns 404 for non-existent ride id
Backend: /refuse returns 403 when called by a passenger (role check)
Backend: /refuse returns 401/403 when called without auth
Backend: Idempotent refuse (calling twice on same pending ride returns 200 or 404 gracefully)
Backend: Both test drivers (driver@test.com=['vtc'], driver3@test.com=['vtc','van']) login correctly
Frontend: Driver dashboard loads at /driver after auth
Frontend: dismiss-ride-{id} button present with correct data-testid
Frontend: Ride card disappears from 'Courses disponibles' immediately after refuse
Frontend: Toast 'Course refusée - Elle sera proposée à un autre chauffeur' shown after refuse
Frontend: After refuse, 'Aucune course disponible pour le moment' empty state shown
Integration: After UI refuse, API GET /rides/available for driver1 returns 0 rides; driver2 still sees 1 ride - filtering works correctly
action_items: NOTE: Task description says driver@test.com is a 'Van' driver, but actual seed data has driver_vehicle_types=['vtc']. If van filtering needs testing, update seed for driver@test.com to include 'van' or use a dedicated van-only driver account.

OPTIONAL: The 5-second asyncio.create_task() reassignment is fire-and-forget; if backend is restarted within 5s after a refusal, the next-driver notification is lost. Consider using a persistent job queue (Celery / Mongo-backed scheduler) for production. Currently this is not a blocker.
critical_code_review_comments: /api/rides/{ride_id}/refuse: Correctly uses $nin filter on refused_by; works for missing field and empty array (verified)
Background reassignment via asyncio.create_task() is non-persistent across backend restarts (server.py:2253) - acceptable for MVP but document as known limitation

dismissRide() in DriverDashboard.js:656 correctly optimistically removes ride from UI, then calls API; on API failure it shows fallback toast 'Course ignorée' but ride is already removed locally - this means a failed /refuse won't restore the card (driver loses ride from his view). Consider re-adding to availableRides on error.
find_nearest_driver in /refuse uses max_distance_km=20.0 - hardcoded; consider env var for tunability
server.py is 4504 lines - exceeds 700-line guideline; should be split into routers (auth, rides, drivers, admin, wallet, payments)
seed_data_creation: None - test creates throwaway rides via API and cancels them in cleanup
context_for_next_testing_agent: Refuse flow fully working end-to-end. localStorage token key is 'volt_token' (not 'token'). Driver availability PUT endpoint is /api/users/availability (not /api/drivers/availability). Vehicle types for test drivers: driver@test.com=['vtc'], driver3@test.com=['vtc','van']. The 5-second async reassignment task (asyncio.create_task) was NOT directly tested by waiting 5s; only the immediate refused_by filtering was verified. If main agent wants to test the 5s reassignment, add a test that sleeps 6s and checks next_driver notification via websocket or notification_manager mock.
rca of the issue: N/A - feature works as expected. Previous implementation issue (unused BackgroundTasks param + missing /refuse call from frontend + missing $nin filter) was correctly fixed per the agent_to_agent_context_note.
